STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL — UAE MARKET ABUSE INSIDE INFORMATION — Project Palm Oasis Term Sheet; embargo until 30 Sep 2026, 08:00 GST.
Parties: Buyer — Al Rimal Holdings DIFC Ltd. (Trade Licence TL-982317, DIFC), Target — Crescent Dunes Utilities PJSC (intended ADX listing Q2 2027), Seller — Noor Coast Investments LLC (Dubai, TL-771204).
Transaction: Acquisition of 35% of Crescent Dunes Utilities PJSC for AED 185,000,000 cash; proceeds to fund Phase II desalination capex; indicative valuation implies AED 528,000,000 post-money.
Structure: Secondary purchase plus new share subscription; settlement via onshore escrow at Al Safa Settlement Services Ltd.; filings anticipated with SCA; DFSA-notified at HoldCo level for inside information governance.
Conditions precedent: regulatory clearances (SCA no-objection, sectoral approvals), DIFC HoldCo board approval, and no Material Adverse Change; for clarity, changes in UAE laws, IFRS updates, industry indices or pandemics declared prior to Signing shall not constitute a MAC.
Definitive agreements: Share Purchase Agreement, Subscription Agreement, and Shareholders’ Agreement (collectively the “Definitive Agreements”); target signing 20 Oct 2026, long-stop date 31 Jan 2027; 2% reverse break fee if cornerstone financing fails.
Governance: Buyer board nomination right proportional to stake (minimum 1 seat); observer rights for Seller until full earn-out satisfied; reserved matters list per ADGM market practice.
Confidentiality/PDPL: personal data will be processed in accordance with UAE PDPL; contact for data queries — Ms. Leila Naser, Chief Legal Officer, Emirates ID 784-1987-1234567-1 (fictional), leila.naser@alrimalholdings.example; note: Mr. Jamil Al-Bajouri represents the founding family, and family-origin disclosure has been consented to for due diligence only.
Funding: Buyer equity from Al Rimal Growth Fund I LP (Cayman feeder mirrored in ADGM SPV), and AED 90,000,000 bridge from Sand Pearl Capital PSC at EIBOR+350 bps.
Use of proceeds: expand Ras Al Noor plant to 180k m3/day; 12% expected IRR uplift; preliminary research note (internal) indicates tariff resilience under regulated framework; not for external distribution.
Investor relations draft (non-public): Q4 call to reference “strategic water security alignment” without naming Target until after embargo; code name to be used in internal calendars only.
Public reference: as per publicly available SCA disclosure guidance, any future announcement will follow ADX timetable and will exclude pricing until signing; nothing in this term sheet constitutes an offer to the public.
Representations and warranties: standard UAE law capacity and title; no undisclosed liens; tax matters limited to onshore VAT; warranty cap 15% of Consideration; survival 18 months; DIFC law and ADGM Arbitration Regulations seat.
Closing/Checklist: KYC for all shareholders (including Emirates ID verification and trade licence extracts), completion of environmental permits transfer, and registration of pledge over subscription shares; closing mechanics per Annex A (internal checklist).
Employee matters: no redundancies planned; retention pool equal to 1.5% fully diluted for key managers; for avoidance of doubt, routine headcount changes due to seasonality shall not be a MAC.
todo: circulate clean spa to counsel by eod; align signing room logistics; and yes, book team spa day post-closing (not a transaction cost).
Non-reliance: this term sheet is non-binding except for confidentiality, costs, exclusivity (45 days), and governing law; any stale data in third-party databases should not be relied upon without refreshed vendor confirmations.
